options.text: Can you write me a prompt for meal prep?
Write a prompt I can use for weekly meal planning that includes the preferences we discussed about high-protein meals and quick cooking times.

Based on our conversation, please write me a reusable prompt that I can use each week for meal prep planning. It should capture all the key requirements we established: high-protein meals, 30-minute maximum cook times, budget-friendly ingredients, and recipes that make good lunch leftovers. Structure it so I can just copy-paste it and get consistent results each time.

responses.response_weak: Sure! Here's a prompt you can use: "Help me plan my meals for the week. I want healthy options that are easy to cook and good for meal prep. Include a grocery list too." Feel free to tweak it however you like!
responses.response_medium:
Here's a meal planning prompt based on what we discussed:

"I need a weekly meal plan focused on high-protein meals that are quick to cook. I want recipes that are practical for meal prep and easy to make on a busy schedule. Please include a variety of options and a shopping list."

This covers the main preferences we talked about — high protein and fast cook times. You can paste this into a new conversation each week and you should get a solid meal plan. Let me know if you want me to adjust anything.
responses.response_strong:
## Your Reusable Weekly Meal Prep Prompt

Here's a copy-paste-ready prompt that captures everything we worked out:

---

**"Plan my weekly meal prep with these requirements:**

- **Protein:** Every meal should have 25-30g of protein per serving
- **Cook time:** Nothing over 30 minutes of active cooking
- **Budget:** Keep ingredients budget-friendly (under $10-12 per serving)
- **Leftovers:** Every dinner recipe must reheat well as next-day lunch

**Please provide:**
1. 5 dinner recipes for the week (with protein count and cook time for each)
2. A batch-cooking game plan for Sunday prep
3. Storage instructions so lunches stay fresh through Wednesday/Thursday
4. A grocery list organized by store section (produce, protein, pantry, dairy)
5. Estimated total grocery cost

**Format each recipe with:** name, protein per serving, active cook time, and a note on how well it stores."

---

### Why This Works
- **Specific numbers** (25-30g protein, 30-min max, $10-12) eliminate guesswork
- **The leftover requirement** ensures every dinner doubles as lunch
- **Organized grocery list** saves time at the store
- **Storage instructions** prevent the "is this still good?" guessing game

Just paste this into any AI tool at the start of each week. You'll get consistent, structured results every time.

feedback.what_happened: The weak prompt gives the AI almost nothing to work with — just 'meal prep' — so it creates a generic template that misses all your specific needs. The medium prompt captures some key details (high-protein, quick cooking) but leaves out crucial elements like the budget constraint and leftover requirement, resulting in an incomplete prompt. The effective prompt transforms your entire conversation into a detailed, copy-paste-ready template with specific numbers, clear formatting instructions, and even explains why each element matters — giving you a tool that will work consistently every week.
feedback.principle: A reusable prompt needs to capture every important detail from your situation — but only the ones that actually affect the output. The strong version includes specific numbers, preferences, and constraints without any filler.
feedback.principle_name: Include everything needed — but nothing extra
feedback.tip: When asking an AI to create something reusable for you, explicitly list every requirement that matters to your situation, including specific numbers or constraints you've discovered through trial and error.
